title: L3: Conditional Functions
description: IF, COUNTIF, SUMIF and percentages for BTEC DIT Component 2 Task 2b.

steps.title: The IF Function
steps.content:
The IF function — make a decision

Sometimes a cell should show different text depending on a value. For example: “if income is above £350, show Top Seller; otherwise show Standard.” That is exactly what IF does.

Syntax — three parts separated by commas:

=IF(condition, value_if_true, value_if_false)

Example: =IF(C2>350, "Top Seller", "Standard")

IncomeCondition C2>350Result
404.55TRUETop Seller
334.33FALSEStandard

Common condition operators:

SymbolMeaning
>Greater than
<Less than
>=Greater than or equal to
=Equal to (inside IF, = means equals)

Your turn

Add =IF(C2>350, "Top Seller", "Standard") in D2 and replicate it down to D3:D6. Then click Check.

Check the condition references the correct column (C) in each row.';return;}fb.className='ex-feedback ok';fb.textContent='Correct! IF automatically applies the right label to each row based on the income.';__markStepComplete();};});});});})();

steps.title: COUNTIF
steps.content:
COUNTIF — count cells that match a condition

=COUNT counts all numbers. =COUNTIF counts only cells that match a specific condition — for example, how many products are sold in the North region.

Syntax:

=COUNTIF(range, criteria)

=COUNTIF(B2:B6, "North") — counts cells in B2:B6 that say “North”

FormulaWhat it counts
=COUNTIF(B2:B6, "North")Cells in B2:B6 containing the text North
=COUNTIF(C2:C6, ">350")Cells in C2:C6 with a value above 350

Your turn

Add COUNTIF formulas in the summary rows:

  • C7: =COUNTIF(B2:B6, "North")
  • C8: =COUNTIF(B2:B6, "South")

North=3 (rows 2,4,6) and South=2 (rows 3,5). Check the criteria match exactly.';return;}fb.className='ex-feedback ok';fb.textContent='Correct! COUNTIF(B2:B6,"North")=3 and COUNTIF(B2:B6,"South")=2. COUNTIF counts by category.';__markStepComplete();};});});});})();

steps.title: SUMIF
steps.content:
SUMIF — add values matching a condition

=SUM adds everything. =SUMIF adds only the values where a corresponding cell matches your condition. For example: the total income from North products only.

Syntax — three parts:

=SUMIF(range_to_check, criteria, range_to_sum)

=SUMIF(B2:B6, "North", C2:C6)

“Where column B says North, add the matching values from column C.”

Your turn

  • C7: =SUMIF(B2:B6, "North", C2:C6) — total North income
  • C8: =SUMIF(B2:B6, "South", C2:C6) — total South income

steps.title: Percentages
steps.content:
Percentages — share of the total

In BTEC you often need to show each product’s income as a percentage of the total:

=B2/B$7*100

“This product’s income ÷ total income × 100”

The $ matters. When you copy this formula down, B2 adjusts to B3, B4, etc. That is fine — each row should reference its own income. But B7 (the total) must never move. Adding $ before the row number locks it: B$7. This is called an absolute reference.

CellFormulaResult
C2=B2/B$7*10023.35%
C3 (copied)=B3/B$7*10027.69%

Your turn

The total is in B7. Type =B2/B$7*100 in C2, then replicate it down to C3:C6. Click Check when all five are done.
steps.js: (function(){function lcss(u){if(!document.querySelector('link[href="'+u+'"]')){var l=document.createElement('link');l.rel='stylesheet';l.href=u;document.head.appendChild(l);}}function ljs(u,cb){var ex=document.querySelector('script[data-jhncc-src="'+u+'"]');if(ex){if(ex.getAttribute('data-loaded')==='1'){cb();}else{ex.addEventListener('load',function(){cb();});}return;}var s=document.createElement('script');s.setAttribute('data-jhncc-src',u);s.src=u;s.onload=function(){s.setAttribute('data-loaded','1');cb();};s.onerror=function(){alert('The spreadsheet tool could not load. Please refresh the page.');};document.head.appendChild(s);}lcss('assets/css/jsuites.css');lcss('assets/css/jspreadsheet.css');ljs('assets/js/jsuites.js',function(){ljs('assets/js/jspreadsheet.js',function(){ljs('assets/js/jspreadsheet-formula-bar.js?v=15',function(){var data=[["Item","Income (£)","% of Total"],["Phone Cases",404.55,""],["Earphones",479.68,""],["Screen Protectors",334.33,""],["Chargers",363.72,""],["Phone Stands",149.85,""],["TOTAL","=SUM(B2:B6)",""]];var sheet=jspreadsheet(document.getElementById('l3-pct-sheet'),{data:data,columns:[{"width":155},{"width":110},{"width":120}],minDimensions:[3,7],tableOverflow:true,tableWidth:'100%',toolbar:false,about:false});JHNCCAddFormulaBar(document.getElementById('l3-pct-sheet'),sheet);JHNCCAddFormatToolbar(document.getElementById('l3-pct-sheet'),sheet);JHNCCAddSheetTabs(document.getElementById('l3-pct-sheet'),sheet);document.getElementById('l3-pct-check').onclick=function(){var fb=document.getElementById('l3-pct-fb');var incomes=[404.55,479.68,334.33,363.72,149.85];var total=1732.13;var ok=0,noFormula=false;for(var row=1;row<=5;row++){var raw=String(sheet.getValueFromCoords(2,row,false)||'');var val=parseFloat(sheet.getValueFromCoords(2,row,true));if(raw.charAt(0)!=='='){noFormula=true;break;}var exp=incomes[row-1]/total*100;if(Math.abs(val-exp)<0.15)ok++;}if(noFormula){fb.className='ex-feedback hint';fb.textContent='Add =B2/B$7*100 in C2 and replicate it down to C3:C6.';return;}if(ok<5){fb.className='ex-feedback err';fb.textContent='Some percentages are wrong. Make sure you used B$7 (not B7) so the total row stays fixed when copying.';return;}fb.className='ex-feedback ok';fb.textContent='Correct! Each product now shows its share of total income. Phone Cases=23.4%, Earphones=27.7%, etc.';__markStepComplete();};});});});})();

steps.title: Full Practice
steps.content:
Full practice — IF, COUNTIF and SUMIF together

Complete all four tasks below using the dataset provided.

TaskCellFormula
1D2:D6=IF(C2>350, "Top Seller", "Standard") — replicate down
2C8=COUNTIF(B2:B6, "North") — North products count
3C9=SUMIF(B2:B6, "South", C2:C6) — South income total
4C10=COUNTIF(C2:C6, ">350") — count high-income products

data=[["Item","Region","Income (£)","Status"],["Phone Cases","North",404.55,""],["Earphones","South",479.68,""],["Screen Protectors","North",334.33,""],["Chargers","South",363.72,""],["Phone Stands","North",149.85,""],["TOTAL","","=SUM(C2:C6)",""],["North count","","",""],["South total","","",""],["Income > 350 count","","",""]];var sheet=jspreadsheet(document.getElementById('l3-all-sheet'),{data:data,columns:[{"width":175},{"width":80},{"width":110},{"width":130}],minDimensions:[4,10],tableOverflow:true,tableWidth:'100%',toolbar:false,about:false});JHNCCAddFormulaBar(document.getElementById('l3-all-sheet'),sheet);JHNCCAddFormatToolbar(document.getElementById('l3-all-sheet'),sheet);JHNCCAddSheetTabs(document.getElementById('l3-all-sheet'),sheet);document.getElementById('l3-all-check').onclick=function(){var fb=document.getElementById('l3-all-fb');var errors=[];var ifOk=0;for(var row=1;row<=5;row++){var raw=String(sheet.getValueFromCoords(3,row,false)||'');var val=String(sheet.getValueFromCoords(3,row,true)||'').toLowerCase().trim();var inc=parseFloat(sheet.getValueFromCoords(2,row,true));if(raw.charAt(0)==='='&&raw.toUpperCase().indexOf('IF(')!==-1&&val===(inc>350?'top seller':'standard'))ifOk++;}if(ifOk<5)errors.push('Task 1: IF in D2:D6 is incomplete or wrong ('+ifOk+'/5 rows correct)');var r8=String(sheet.getValueFromCoords(2,7,false)||'');var v8=parseFloat(sheet.getValueFromCoords(2,7,true));if(r8.toUpperCase().indexOf('COUNTIF')===-1)errors.push('Task 2: use COUNTIF in C8');else if(Math.abs(v8-3)>0.5)errors.push('Task 2: North count should be 3, got '+v8);var r9=String(sheet.getValueFromCoords(2,8,false)||'');var v9=parseFloat(sheet.getValueFromCoords(2,8,true));if(r9.toUpperCase().indexOf('SUMIF')===-1)errors.push('Task 3: use SUMIF in C9');else if(Math.abs(v9-843.40)>0.5)errors.push('Task 3: South total should be 843.40, got '+v9);var r10=String(sheet.getValueFromCoords(2,9,false)||'');var v10=parseFloat(sheet.getValueFromCoords(2,9,true));if(r10.toUpperCase().indexOf('COUNTIF')===-1)errors.push('Task 4: use COUNTIF in C10');else if(Math.abs(v10-3)>0.5)errors.push('Task 4: count of income>350 should be 3, got '+v10);if(errors.length){fb.className='ex-feedback err';fb.textContent='Issues: '+errors.join(' | ');return;}fb.className='ex-feedback ok';fb.textContent='All four tasks correct! IF, COUNTIF and SUMIF are the core conditional functions in BTEC Task 2b.';__markStepComplete();};});});});})();

steps.title: Check What You Have Learned
steps.content:
Check what you have learned

Feedback appears after each question.
steps.js: createMCQSet('l3-exam',[{q:'=IF(B2>100, "Pass", "Fail"). B2=80. What is returned?',options:['Fail','Pass','80','TRUE'],answer:0,right:'Correct — 80>100 is FALSE so the third part is returned.'},{q:'What does =COUNTIF(B2:B10, "North") do?',options:['Counts cells in B2:B10 that contain North','Adds up values where B says North','Checks if North exists in B2','Returns the position of North'],answer:0,right:'Correct — COUNTIF counts matching cells.'},{q:'=SUMIF(B2:B6, "South", C2:C6). What does this add?',options:['C values where the matching B cell says South','All values in C2:C6','B values that say South','All values in both columns'],answer:0,right:'Correct — SUMIF adds C values where B matches the criteria.'},{q:'You copy =B2/B$7*100 from C2 to C3. What does C3 contain?',options:['=B3/B$7*100','=B3/B8*100','=B2/B$7*100','=B3/B7*100'],answer:0,right:'Correct — B3 adjusts (relative) but B$7 stays locked (absolute).'},{q:'Which formula counts cells in D2:D10 with a value greater than 500?',options:['=COUNTIF(D2:D10,">500")','=COUNT(D2:D10,">500")','=COUNTIF(D2:D10,500)','=IF(D2:D10>500)'],answer:0,right:'Correct — the criteria goes in quotes: ">500".'},{q:'What is the purpose of $ in B$7?',options:['Locks row 7 so it does not change when the formula is copied down','Formats the cell as currency','Indicates a negative value','Separates arguments'],answer:0,right:'Correct — $ locks that part of the reference.'}]);

steps.title: Endless Practice
steps.content:
Endless practice

Answer 5 in a row to finish. A wrong answer resets the streak.
steps.js:
(function(){var bank=[{q:'=IF(A2>50,"Yes","No"). A2=30. What is returned?',o:['No','Yes','30','TRUE'],a:0},{q:'=IF(A2>50,"Yes","No"). A2=70. What is returned?',o:['Yes','No','70','FALSE'],a:0},{q:'Which function counts cells matching a condition?',o:['COUNTIF','COUNT','SUMIF','IF'],a:0},{q:'Which function sums only cells where a condition is met?',o:['SUMIF','SUM','COUNTIF','IF'],a:0},{q:'=SUMIF(B2:B6,"East",C2:C6). What does it add up?',o:['C values where B says East','B values where B says East','All of C2:C6','All of B2:B6'],a:0},{q:'How do you lock row 7 in a cell reference?',o:['B$7','$B7','B7$','$$7'],a:0},{q:'=COUNTIF(D2:D20,">100"). What does this count?',o:['Cells in D2:D20 with a value above 100','Cells equal to 100','All numeric cells','Cells below 100'],a:0},{q:'In =IF(condition, A, B), when is B returned?',o:['When the condition is FALSE','When the condition is TRUE','Always','Never'],a:0},{q:'You copy =C2/C$7*100 from D2 to D3. What does D3 contain?',o:['=C3/C$7*100','=C2/C$7*100','=C3/C8*100','=C3/C7*100'],a:0}];var solved=0,c=document.getElementById('l3-endless');function pick(){return bank[Math.floor(Math.random()*bank.length)];}function render(){var q=pick();var opts=q.o.map(function(x,i){return{x:x,i:i};}).sort(function(){return Math.random()-0.5;});c.innerHTML='

Correct in a row: '+solved+' / 5

'+q.q+'

'+opts.map(function(o){return '';}).join('')+'
';c.querySelectorAll('button').forEach(function(btn){btn.onclick=function(){var fb=c.querySelector('.ex-feedback');if(parseInt(btn.dataset.i)===q.a){solved++;fb.className='ex-feedback ok';fb.textContent=solved>=5?'Practice complete! Well done.':'Correct! Next question.';if(solved>=5)__markStepComplete();else setTimeout(render,700);}else{solved=0;fb.className='ex-feedback err';fb.textContent='Not quite — streak reset. Try again.';setTimeout(render,1100);}};});} render();})();

quizQuestions.q: In =IF(C2>350,"Top Seller","Standard"), what is returned when C2 is 334.33?
Use IF in D2 and D3. If Income is above 350, show Top Seller. Otherwise show Standard.
Complete D4:D6 using IF. If Income is above 350, show Top Seller. Otherwise show Standard.

options: Cells in B2:B6 that contain North

quizQuestions.q: Use COUNTIF in C7 to count how many products are in the North region.
Use SUMIF in C8 to add the Income values for products in the South region.
Use COUNTIF in C9 to count how many Income values are greater than 350.
The total is in B7. In C2, calculate Phone Cases as a percentage of the total using an absolute reference to row 7.
